summary: Tight Felt Forum set with soaring Truckin', Mr. Charlie & epic Other One jam
blurb: The band delivers a tight, high‑energy performance highlighted by stellar versions of Truckin’, Mr. Charlie and an expansive The Other One > Me And Bobby McGee > The Other One sequence. Jerry’s soaring solos and Keith’s piano drive the jams while Phil anchors with solid bass. Overall a consistently strong set captured on a crisp soundboard recording.
review:
On December 6, 1971 at New York’s Felt Forum the Grateful Dead turned in a remarkably tight show that still feels fresh decades later. The first set bursts out of the gate with a rollicking Truckin’ and a soulful Loser before diving into a crisp Mr. Charlie and a punchy Jack Straw, then glides seamlessly through the classic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider pairing and a festive Run Rudolph Run. Highlights continue in the second set where Phil’s bass locks in with Jerry and Keith during an expansive The Other One > Me And Bobby McGee > The Other One jam that showcases the band’s evolving cohesion, while Pigpen’s organ adds texture despite occasional mic hiccups. The night closes on a heartfelt Wharf Rat and a sing‑along Uncle John’s Band, all captured on two solid soundboard tapes that preserve the energy without major flaws.

Both available recordings are soundboard sources with good clarity; the Kaplan tape (gd71-12-06.sbd.kaplan.2418.sbeok.shnf) is praised for its overall crispness despite a few piano mic distortions and a slight kick‑drum buzz, while the Miller tape (gd1971-12-06.sbd.miller.108425.flac16) offers minimal noise but splits the Cryptical/Drums/The Other One segment into separate tracks, which some fans find distracting. Either way, listeners get an excellent window onto a night where Jerry’s soaring solos, Keith’s tasteful keyboards, and Phil’s steady bass combine for one of the tighter 1971 performances.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Both recordings are described with positive sentiment, repeatedly praising standout songs (Truckin', Mr. Charlie, The Other One jam), noting tight set flow and strong individual performances. No negative commentary on the musical performance appears across analyses.

best_recording.reason: Clear soundboard capture with overall crispness; minor piano mic issues are outweighed by superb band balance and completeness of the set.

key_highlights: Epic The Other One > Me And Bobby McGee > The Other One jam showcases tight group interplay
Crisp soundboard capture despite occasional piano mic distortion and kick‑drum buzz
Strong vocal performances on Jack Straw, Me And My Uncle, and the closing Uncle John’s Band

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivered soaring solos throughout, especially on Truckin', Mr. Charlie and Black Peter, and his interplay during the extended jams was a highlight.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh provided a solid bass foundation and locked in with Jerry and Keith during the big The Other One jam; some listeners wished his mix was a bit louder but his playing remained tight.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocal work shone on Jack Straw and Me And My Uncle, adding punch to the set.
band_performance.Keith: Keith’s piano/organ added texture and drive; despite occasional mic distortion his touch was praised, especially in the jam sections.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey kept a steady pulse, complementing Bill’s drumming without any noticeable issues.
band_performance.Billy: Bill’s drum work stayed tight throughout, with only brief solo moments that left fans wanting more extended passages.
